title: [lichen_l2] secure_zero of rx_link_key can be skipped on early return
description:
Cross-file security bug: In lichen_l2_input(), the rx_link_key local buffer is not zeroed on ALL return paths.

The rx_link_key buffer (line 1134) contains a copy of the link key. It is zeroed:
1. On lichen_link_rx failure (line 1169)  
2. On ipv6_len overflow check (line 1177)
3. On successful path completion (line 1188)

But if the link_ctx_initialized check fails (line 1109), the function returns early at line 1112 WITHOUT zeroing rx_link_key.

Wait - at line 1109, rx_link_key hasn't been declared yet! The declaration is at line 1134, which is AFTER the link_ctx_initialized check.

So this is NOT a bug - the early returns at lines 1109-1112 happen before rx_link_key is even declared/initialized.

Closing as not-a-bug.
